steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The word “guiding” is underlined. The Rat hears a cry and uses the sound to navigate through the dark woods to reach the beech tree. The word must capture how sound directs his movement.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Withdrawing: Withdrawing means pulling back or retreating. The Rat is moving toward the sound, not away from it. ✗
  • Choice B — Presenting: Presenting means displaying or offering something. The Rat is not displaying the sound — he is using it to navigate. ✗
  • Choice C — Instructing: Instructing means teaching. The Rat is not being taught by the sound; he is using it to find his way. ✗
  • Choice D — Steering: Steering means directing one's course or path. Plug in: “Steering himself by the sound” = using the sound to direct his movement toward the tree. This matches the context exactly. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
